title: Columnea cerropirrana
taxonomy: Columnea cerropirrana is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the gesneriad or African violet family. The Gesneriaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Columneae and Episcieae. Columnea cerropirrana is a part of the Columneae subfamily, which is primarily composed of woody plants native to Central and South America. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Columneae subfamily.

More studies are required to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.

intro: Columnea cerropirrana, also known as the Cerro Pirre Columnea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central America. This plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various infections and inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Columnea cerropirrana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Columnea cerropirrana is used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems.
